failure_analysis: The diagnostics show active failure modes: single_component_dominance, repeated_event_exploitation, and shaping_goal_mismatch. The dominant component is r_contact, an event_bonus giving +20 per contact event, with a ratio of 0.922. The contact event toggles 39 times, likely due to the lander bouncing or repeatedly touching the ground, causing huge cumulative reward and overshadowing all shaping components. The shaping penalties for distance, velocity, angle, and energy are too small relative to the exploitable event bonus, leading to goal mismatch and dominance.
memory_usage: No relevant memory cards retrieved; edit based entirely on current diagnostics.
operator_reasoning: convert_to_one_time_event is explicitly allowed for event_bonus components and directly targets repeated event exploitation by granting the landing bonus only the first time contact occurs, rather than on every toggle. This will eliminate the reward loop from bouncing and reduce r_contact's dominance while preserving the intended incentive to land.
diagnosis: Repeated contact event exploitation (bouncing) inflates r_contact, creating single-component dominance. Converting r_contact to a one-time event resolves this.
